header.title: Heading Structure Analyzer — Audit H1-H6 Hierarchy for SEO
header.description: Free online heading structure analyzer to audit the H1-H6 hierarchy of your HTML content. Detect missing headings, improper nesting, multiple H1 tags, and empty headings. Ensure proper heading structure for better SEO rankings and WCAG accessibility compliance.
header.keywords: heading structure analyzer, h1 h2 h3 checker, heading hierarchy audit, seo heading checker, html heading analyzer, accessibility heading audit

input.placeholder: Paste HTML or text content here...

issues.title: Issues Found
issues.skipLevel: Skipped heading level (e.g., H1 to H3)
issues.longHeading: Heading is {length} chars — consider shortening to under 70
issues.duplicateMessage: Duplicate H{level}: "{text}" appears more than once.
issues.goodStructure: Heading structure looks good! Proper H1–H6 hierarchy with no major issues.
issues.noH1Found: No H1 heading found. Every page should have exactly one H1 for SEO.
issues.multipleH1Found: Multiple H1 headings ({count}). Best practice: use only one H1 per page.
issues.firstNotH1: First heading is H{level}, not H1. Consider starting with H1 for proper hierarchy.
issues.skipLevelFound: H{prevLevel} → H{level} skips a level (missing H{missing}). Found: "{text}"

tips.title: SEO Tips
tips.oneH1: Use only one H1 per page
tips.primaryKeyword: Include your primary keyword in the H1
tips.contentHierarchy: Headings help Google understand content hierarchy
tips.usersScan: Users scan headings — make them descriptive
tips.headingLength: Heading length: H1 ≤ 70 chars, H2 ≤ 60 chars
tips.h2Section: H2s are your main section headings — use 2–5 per page

emptyState.instruction: Paste HTML or Markdown on the left
emptyState.description: The tool will extract and visualize your H1–H6 heading structure with SEO analysis

howToUse.title: How to Use Heading Structure Analyzer
howToUse.step1Desc: Paste HTML or Markdown content into the input area
howToUse.step2Desc: Select HTML or Markdown mode depending on your content type
howToUse.step3Desc: Click Analyze to extract all H1-H6 headings and detect issues
howToUse.step4Desc: Check for missing H1, skipped levels, empty headings, or duplicate headings

examples.result: Good structure — sequential hierarchy with no skipped levels

items.question: Why is heading structure important for SEO?
items.answer: Proper heading structure (H1-H6) helps search engines understand the hierarchy and relevance of your content, improving readability and keyword relevance signals.

items.question: How many H1 tags should a page have?
items.answer: Best practice is to use one H1 tag per page that describes the main topic. Multiple H1s can confuse search engines about the primary subject.

items.question: What is a heading structure skip?
items.answer: A heading skip occurs when you jump from an H2 to an H4 without an H3 in between. This breaks the logical hierarchy and can confuse both users and search engines.

items.question: Can headings contain keywords?
items.answer: Yes, using relevant keywords in your headings helps search engines understand your content structure and can improve rankings for those terms.
